userStories.title: Project Setup & Calculation Engine
userStories.description:
Configure the React+Vite+Tailwind project with a dark theme matching the showcase (bg: #0f1523, cards: #1a2236, text: #f1f3f7, accent: #4f8cff, green: #10b981, red: #ef4444). No Luzia design system — use a custom dark financial dashboard look.

Setup:
- Configure Tailwind with the dark color palette above
- Set up src/index.css with global dark styles
- Create src/lib/financial.ts with PURE FUNCTIONS (no React) for all calculations:
  1. frenchAmortization(principal, annualRate, termMonths) → { monthlyPayment, schedule: [{month, payment, principal, interest, balance}] }
  2. depreciateValue(purchasePrice, carAgeYears, brandTier: 'premium'|'standard', yearsForward) → residualValue
     - Premium brands (BMW, Audi, Volvo, Lexus, Mercedes): -15% Y1, -10% Y2, -8% Y3, -7% Y4, -5% Y5+
     - Standard: -20% Y1, -12% Y2, -10% Y3, -8% Y4, -6% Y5+
     - Floor: 10% of original price
     - Account for car's current age (a 2020 car in 2026 has already depreciated 6 years)
  3. computeCashflow(carData, params) → { months: [{month, items: {downPayment, itp, gestoria, loanPayment, insurance, fuel, service, itv, residual}, total}], npv }
  4. computeRentingCashflow(params) → { months: [{month, items: {rentFee}, total}], npv }
  5. computeNPV(cashflows: number[], monthlyDiscountRate) → number
- Create src/lib/costModel.ts with the Spanish cost model data baked in (from config/cost-model.json):
  - fuel_monthly rates by fuel type
  - insurance_monthly rates by brand + HP surcharge
  - service_annual rates by brand + EV override
- Create src/types.ts with TypeScript interfaces for CarData, FinancialParams, CashflowRow, CashflowResult
- Verify npm run build passes with no errors

userStories.title: Car Data Ingestion & Parameter State
userStories.description:
Read car data from URL query parameters and set up the reactive parameter state.

URL params the app receives (all from the showcase CTA):
- make, model, variant, year, price, mileage, fuel, hp, transmission, platform, image, link

Create src/hooks/useCarData.ts:
- Parse URL search params into a CarData object
- Determine brandTier ('premium' for BMW/Audi/Volvo/Lexus/Mercedes, 'standard' for rest)
- Look up insurance, fuel, service rates from costModel

Create src/hooks/useFinancialParams.ts:
- React state for ALL configurable parameters with defaults:
  - loanAmount: 80% of car price
  - interestRate: 7 (APR %)
  - loanTerm: 60 (months)
  - rentingFee: 350 (€/month)
  - rentingPeriod: 48 (months)
  - timeHorizon: 120 (months = 10 years)
  - discountRate: 5 (%)
  - annualKm: 15000
  - itpRate: 4 (%)
  - gestoriaFees: 300 (€)
  - itvCost: 40 (€/year)
- Return params + setParam(key, value) function
- Memoize: recompute purchase + renting cashflows whenever params OR car data change using useMemo

Create src/App.tsx:
- Use both hooks
- Display a simple car info header (make, model, variant, year, price, mileage, fuel, HP, image)
- Show a placeholder for the cashflow table area
- Verify the app loads correctly with URL params like: ?make=BMW&model=Serie+5&variant=520dA+Touring&year=2020&price=23290&mileage=109186&fuel=Diesel&hp=190

userStories.title: Purchase Cashflow Spreadsheet Table
userStories.description:
Build the core spreadsheet-style cashflow table for the PURCHASE scenario.

Create src/components/CashflowTable.tsx:
- Spreadsheet layout: rows = line items, columns = months (M0, M1, M2, ... up to timeHorizon)
- Columns grouped by year with year headers (Y1, Y2, Y3...) spanning their 12 months
- Frozen first column (item names) — stays visible while scrolling horizontally
- Horizontal scroll for the months columns

Rows (purchase scenario):
1. Down Payment — negative value in M0 only (= price - loanAmount)
2. Transfer Tax (ITP) — negative in M0 (= price × itpRate)
3. Gestoría — negative in M0
4. Loan Installment — negative monthly during loan term, then 0
5. Insurance — negative every month for full horizon
6. Fuel — negative every month for full horizon
7. Service — negative in M12, M24, M36... (annual)
8. ITV — negative annually, only when car age ≥ 4 years (car year + month offset)
9. Residual Value — positive in the LAST month only (depreciated sale price)
10. **Monthly Total** — sum of all items for that month
11. **Cumulative Total** — running sum

Styling:
- Negative values in red (#ef4444), positive in green (#10b981)
- Alternating row backgrounds for readability
- Year header rows with accent background (#4f8cff at 20% opacity)
- Monospace font for numbers, right-aligned
- Compact cells — this is a dense financial spreadsheet, not a pretty dashboard
- All values formatted as integers with € symbol

The table receives the cashflow result from computeCashflow() and renders it. It should NOT do any calculations itself.

userStories.title: Renting Cashflow Table & NPV Summary
userStories.description:
Add the renting cashflow table and the NPV comparison summary.

Renting table (same CashflowTable component, different data):
- Single row: Monthly Rent Fee — negative for rentingPeriod months, then 0
- Monthly Total and Cumulative Total rows
- After renting period ends, remaining months in horizon show 0
- Uses the same styling as purchase table

NPV Summary section (src/components/NPVSummary.tsx):
- Three cards in a row:
  1. Purchase NPV: value in € (includes all outflows + residual inflow, discounted)
  2. Renting NPV: value in € (just monthly fees, discounted)
  3. Delta: Purchase NPV - Renting NPV, with label indicating which is cheaper
- Color the delta card: green if purchase wins, red if renting wins
- Show the discount rate used
- Below the cards, add a one-line explanation: 'NPV compares the total cost of each option in today's money, using a X% annual discount rate'

Layout order on the page:
1. Car info header
2. Purchase cashflow table (with section title 'Purchase Scenario')
3. Renting cashflow table (with section title 'Renting Scenario')
4. NPV Summary

Verify all numbers are internally consistent: the NPV should equal the discounted sum of the monthly totals shown in each table.

userStories.title: Parameters Panel
userStories.description:
Build the configurable parameters panel as a collapsible sidebar.

Create src/components/ParametersPanel.tsx:
- Fixed-position sidebar on the right side, collapsible via a toggle button (gear icon or 'Parameters' label)
- When collapsed: just the toggle button visible
- When expanded: overlay panel with all parameter inputs

Parameter groups:
1. **Loan** — Loan amount (€ input, step 500), Interest rate (% input, step 0.5), Loan term (months dropdown: 12/24/36/48/60/72/84)
2. **Renting** — Monthly fee (€ input, step 25), Contract period (months dropdown: 12/24/36/48/60)
3. **General** — Time horizon (years dropdown: 3/5/7/10/15), Discount rate (% input, step 0.5), Annual km (input, step 1000)
4. **Taxes & Fees** — ITP rate (% input), Gestoría (€ input), ITV cost (€/year input)

Behavior:
- Every input change immediately updates the React state from useFinancialParams
- This triggers useMemo recomputation of both cashflows
- Tables and NPV update instantly — NO submit button, NO debounce needed
- Show the current car's insurance and fuel rates as read-only info (from cost model)

Styling:
- Dark panel matching the theme
- Compact inputs, labels above
- Group headers with subtle dividers
- The panel should not push the main content — it overlays on top

userStories.title: Car Comparison
userStories.description:
Allow picking a second car to compare financial models side-by-side.

Data source:
- The app receives a 'cars' URL param containing a base64-encoded JSON array of all cars from the showcase
- Alternatively, a 'carsUrl' param pointing to a JSON file (for when the list is too large for URL)
- Parse this into an array of CarData objects

Create src/components/CarPicker.tsx:
- A dropdown/searchable select at the bottom of the page: 'Compare with another car'
- Shows available cars as: Make Model Variant (Year) — €Price
- When a car is selected, compute its full cashflow with the SAME parameters

Comparison display:
- Show the second car's cashflow tables side by side (or stacked below the first)
- Add a comparison row to the NPV Summary: both cars' purchase NPVs + renting NPVs + deltas
- Highlight which car is the better financial deal

The comparison should use the same parameter values for both cars (same loan terms, horizon, discount rate). Only car-specific values differ (price, insurance rate, fuel rate, service rate, depreciation curve).

Include a 'Remove comparison' button to go back to single-car view.

userStories.title: Showcase Integration & Polish
userStories.description:
This story is about preparing the integration point. The actual showcase.html template modification will be done outside Ralph.

In the financial model app:
- Add a 'Back to Showcase' link/button in the header that uses window.history.back() or a configurable backUrl param
- Add a page title: 'Financial Model — {Make} {Model}'
- Ensure the app handles missing URL params gracefully (show an error message if no car data provided)
- Make sure the app works when opened directly at localhost:5173/?make=BMW&model=Serie+5&... for development
- Run npm run build and verify the dist/ output works as static files
- Test with at least 2 different car configurations to verify calculations adapt correctly

Polish:
- Ensure horizontal scroll on the cashflow table works smoothly
- Verify all numbers format correctly (€ symbol, thousands separator, no decimals for integers, 2 decimals for rates)
- Check that the parameters panel doesn't obstruct the tables on smaller screens
- Add print-friendly styles (@media print) that expand the full table without horizontal scroll
